title: portulacaceae
intro: The Portulacaceae rank, also known as the purslane family, is a taxonomic rank that refers to a group of flowering plants characterized by their small, often succulent leaves and stems. This family is primarily composed of annuals and perennials, with a few species being shrubs or vines. Members of the Portulacaceae family can be found in a wide range of habitats, from deserts and dry scrublands to grasslands and rocky outcrops. The Portulacaceae family includes approximately 50 genera and over 1,000 species, many of which are native to the Americas, Australia, and parts of Europe.
